rules.name: deployment
rules.overview: Production deployment guidance: Dockerfile usage, REST vs MCP trade-offs, scaling, and network egress requirements. Read this at Phase 5.

mode.name: library_in_process
mode.when: The agent is a Python service and you control its container image.
mode.how: Add the package to your agent's pyproject.toml or requirements.txt. Build evaluator once at app startup, reuse per request.
mode.pros: Lowest latency (no HTTP hop). Single deployable.
mode.cons: Pinned to Python; can't share the evaluator across non-Python services.

mode.name: rest_sidecar
mode.when: Agent is non-Python, or you want to share guardrails across multiple services.
mode.how: Deploy `real-time-guardrails serve --port 8090` as a separate container/process. Agent calls /api/evaluate over HTTP.
mode.pros: Language-agnostic. Independent scaling. Hot-reload guardrails without redeploying agent.
mode.cons: +5-20 ms per request (localhost) or +50-200 ms (cross-AZ). Extra deployable to operate.
mode.best_for: Polyglot teams, multi-tenant deployments where guardrails policy changes more often than agent code.

mode.name: mcp_tool
mode.when: Agent is built on MCP and uses tools.
mode.how: Register `real-time-guardrails` as an MCP server in the agent's MCP config. Agent calls evaluate_* tools.
mode.pros: Agent-native integration. Natural fit for any MCP-capable host application — chat clients, IDE extensions, agent frameworks — that discovers and invokes MCP server tools.
mode.cons: MCP transport overhead. Doesn't fit non-MCP agents.

run.command: docker run -d \ --name guardrails \ -p 8090:8080 \ -e WATSONX_APIKEY="$WATSONX_APIKEY" \ -e WXG_SERVICE_INSTANCE_ID="$WXG_SERVICE_INSTANCE_ID" \ -e WXG_PROJECT_ID="$WXG_PROJECT_ID" \ real-time-guardrails:0.1.0

kubernetes.use_secrets: Mount credentials via Kubernetes Secrets, never bake into the image.

scaling.stateless: The REST server is stateless per request. Horizontal scaling via N replicas is straightforward.
scaling.load_balancer: Round-robin LB in front of N replicas. Each request is independent (no session affinity needed).
scaling.bottleneck: The bottleneck is the upstream IBM Cloud service, not the package itself. Granite Guardian and watsonx.ai have their own rate limits — check your IBM Cloud plan.
scaling.connection_pooling: The SDK reuses HTTP connections per evaluator instance. Build one evaluator per worker process, not per request.

required_endpoints.endpoint: watsonx.governance service endpoint (region-specific) — used by Granite Guardian and all SDK-backed metrics.
*.ml.cloud.ibm.com (region-specific) — used by watsonx.ai LLM-judge metrics only.

network_egress.air_gap: Not supported. The SDK cannot run offline.
network_egress.egress_proxy: If your environment requires outbound proxy, set HTTPS_PROXY env var before starting the server. The SDK respects standard requests-library proxy env vars.
network_egress.vpc_co_location: Recommended: deploy guardrails service in the same region/VPC as the agent to minimize latency.

pattern.name: single_evaluator_per_call_policy
pattern.how: One service, one evaluator instance. Pass thresholds + fallback_messages per request based on partner_id.
pattern.when: Many partners, similar metric set, different thresholds.

pattern.name: evaluator_per_partner
pattern.how: One service, N evaluators at startup (one per partner with different threshold_overrides=).
pattern.when: Few partners, very different policies, want isolation.

pattern.name: service_per_partner
pattern.how: N services, each with its own evaluator + YAML config.
pattern.when: Strict isolation required (separate billing, blast radius).

upgrades.sdk_version_bumps: The IBM SDK (`ibm-watsonx-gov`) ships breaking changes between minor versions. When upgrading: re-run integration tests against staging credentials before promoting to prod.
upgrades.metric_catalog_changes: When the SDK adds new metrics (or renames columns), the package needs an update. The maintainer's sync workflow (see assets/sdk/CONTRIBUTING.md) covers this.
upgrades.threshold_calibration: Re-calibrate thresholds quarterly against fresh sample data. Granite Guardian model updates can shift score distributions.

observability_basics.metric: p50, p95, p99 latency (per choke point)
llm_judge_token_count (if WXG_PROJECT_ID is set, for cost attribution)

production_checklist.item: Credentials in Kubernetes Secrets / equivalent, never in image.
Outbound HTTPS to required endpoints allowlisted by network policy.
Dashboards: block/flag rate, latency, error rate, LLM cost.
Alerting: block-rate spike, upstream error spike, p95 latency regression.
Threshold policies version-controlled (YAML files in a repo).
Failure mode documented: what happens when guardrails are degraded? (Fail-open vs fail-closed — partner's call.)
